family: train_deriv (deriv7_<variant>: cond_local, freeW, lean-stencil S-sweep, hygiene)
authored: 2026-07-08, Fable, per CHARTER v1.3 I18d; incident 1827034 is the proof case
config: lr 5e-5, 300 ep, --rel-floor 0.1, f64, batch 4, filtered splits, pool per I6

plateau.val_Nddot: ~0.27 stable from ep0 (the rollout-ceiling metric; the number to watch)
plateau.pooled_convention: the '0.19 plateau' quoted in ledgers is the floored/median Nddot convention; pooled-val includes near/past-wall samples and reads O(0.1-1)

known_metric_caveat: PRE-F1: the control's own val_Ndot POOLED MEAN oscillates 0.28-8.9 over ep0-11 while its MEDIAN sits ~0.2 -- a few near-zero-denominator val samples dominate the unfloored eval mean. Until the F1 fix (eval denominator floored like the loss, or per-order MEDIAN reported alongside), ORDER-INVERSION will fire on val_Ndot for ANY run of this family, INCLUDING a healthy one. That firing is intentional: it forces the F1 metric fix into the resubmission rather than letting best-ckpt selection ride a broken number. POST-F1: recalibrate this card's control rows from the first F1-metric control run, then inversion firings are real.
notes: medians 0.19/0.26/0.33 = healthy-window physics-init (width-15 spatial gap), diagnostics/RESULTS_2026-07-03.md
S=4 lock reference (0.026/0.031/0.043) is dt=1e-3 single-grid -- NOT comparable to S=7 sweep vals
epoch walltime ~590-600 s on ibgpu (control); first val row lands ~16 min after epoch loop start
per-run acceptance is separate from this card (e.g. cond_local: rel_Nddot(t=0) on kf4@1.5e-2 in 0.023-0.05 at eval time)
